title: capsicum fasciculatum
intro: Capsicum fasciculatum, a species of flowering plant in the nightshade family, is native to Australia and is known for its bright red or yellow berries. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and prevent infections. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the berries of Capsicum fasciculatum are often dried and made into teas, tinctures, or infusions to harness their medicinal properties.
